etymology.description: Depicts an old man with long hair -> signifies length or longevity
etymology.interpretation: The character 長(cháng, "long") represents an old man with long hair, capturing the association of long hair with age and perceived longevity. This combines to convey the concept of 'long' or 'length' as well as the idea of being 'senior.'

examples.chinese: 这条街很长(這條街很長)。
他的头发又黑又长(他的頭髮又黑又長)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de tóufà yòu hēi yòu cháng.
examples.english: His hair is black and long.

examples.chinese: 长城是世界著名的地标(長城是世界著名的地標)。
examples.pinyin: Chángchéng shì shìjiè zhùmíng de dìbiāo.
examples.english: The Great Wall is a world-famous landmark.
